payload.description: MRT-2 escalator payments totaling $6.424 million to Dutchess, Orange and Rockland Counties

evidence_refs.source_quote: Mr. Keller informed the Finance Committee and MTA Board about the statutorily required MRT-2 escalator payments, as stipulated in Section 1270-a(4)(c) of the Public Authorities Law, to Dutchess, Orange and Rockland Counties totaling $6.424 million, which are expected to be made in March 2024.

payload.raw_value_text: Operating Subsidies were $19 million, or 6%, favorable
Operating Expenses were favorable by $13 million (1%)
Passenger revenues were $125 million (26%) below farebox revenue in January 2019
toll revenue exceeded January 2019 toll revenue by $10 million (7%)
Debt Service Expense was $1 million (<1%) favorable

payload.description: Triborough Bridge and Tunnel Authority Sales Tax Revenue Bonds, Series 2024A

payload.claim_text: A request to the Board to approve the award of a 10-year Original Equipment Manufacturer purchase agreement for M7 Propulsion System Equipment Upgrade Overhaul and Bench Test Equipment to Mitsubishi Electric Power Products, Inc.
payload.subject: Board procurement request
payload.description: 10-year OEM purchase agreement for M7 Propulsion System Equipment Upgrade Overhaul and Bench Test Equipment to Mitsubishi Electric Power Products, Inc.

payload.claim_text: A request the Board approve a five-year contract for HVAC and propulsion replacement parts utilized on LIRR's M-7 electric railcars and Metro-North's M-7 and M-8 electric railcars. This will also include NYC Transit's subway car needs.
payload.subject: Board procurement request
payload.description: Five-year contract for HVAC and propulsion replacement parts for LIRR M-7, Metro-North M-7 and M-8 electric railcars, and NYC Transit subway car needs, awarded to Mitsubishi Electric Power Products, Inc.

payload.claim_text: The 2024 Adopted Budget remains at $19.3 billion, is balanced annually through 2027, and is the same as was presented in November 2023 and adopted in December 2023.
payload.subject: MTA 2024 Adopted Budget

payload.description: 2024 Adopted Budget
MRT-2 escalator payments to Dutchess, Orange and Rockland Counties

payload.raw_value_text: favorable Petroleum Business Tax (PBT) of $36 million
payload.description: December 2023 receipts not transferred to the MTA until early January
Passenger revenues were slightly below the midpoint (70.8% vs. 71.7%) when compared with January 2019
Discussed at MTA Finance Committee meeting; proceeds from TBTA bonds to finance a portion of the capital costs of the Central Business District Tolling Program

evidence_refs.source_quote: Mr. Willens spoke briefly on the Central Business District Tolling Program and its impact on MTA finances.

payload.description: Meeting of the Metropolitan Transportation Authority Finance Committee at 2 Broadway, 20th Floor Board Room, New York, NY
The minutes of the meeting held on January 29, 2024, were approved.

payload.claim_text: An action item to obtain MTA Board approval of resolutions to (1) certify and transfer $1.289 billion of TBTA operating surplus to the MTA and NYCT pursuant to Section 1219-a(2)(b) of the Public Authorities Law; (2) transfer $12.4 million representing 2023 investment income to the MTA pursuant to Section 569-c of the Public Authorities Law; and (3) advance the 2024 TBTA Surplus.
payload.subject: TBTA surplus transfer

payload.description: TBTA operating surplus to be certified and transferred to the MTA and NYCT
2023 investment income to be transferred to the MTA pursuant to Section 569-c of the Public Authorities Law
Proceeds from TBTA Sales Tax Revenue Bonds, Series 2024A will finance a portion of the capital costs of the Central Business District Tolling Program

evidence_refs.source_quote: Proceeds from the transaction will be used to finance ... a portion of the capital costs of the Central Business District Tolling Program

issues: evidence_refs[0].source_quote is not present in the cited block

payload.description: The MTA Finance Committee held the meeting on February 26, 2024
Proceeds from TBTA Sales Tax Revenue Bonds, Series 2024A will finance a portion of the capital costs of the Central Business District Tolling Program
Proceeds from TBTA Sales Tax Revenue Bonds, Series 2024A will be used to finance approved 2020-2024 Capital Program transit and commuter projects
